observations.recommendation:
1.11 We recommended that:
a. The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) in coordination with the Municipal Health Officer strictly adhere to the Drug Price Reference Index (DPRI) during bid evaluation to prevent overpricing; and
b. The Municipal Health Officer and the BAC ensure that all purchase requests and all purchase orders clearly indicate the complete quantities and unit measurements of medicines to be procured, particularly the specific contents per box.

2.15 In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee:
a. Ensure compliance with Section 18 of RA No. 9184 by removing any reference to brand names in the bidding documents and bidder disqualifications, except in specific instances permitted by law;
b. Duly notify bidders of all procurement stages requiring their attention to promote transparency and fair competition; and
c. Prepare the Bidding Documents and ensure that they are properly received by the bidders, particularly when additional requirements are involved and such should be submitted to the Audit Team as part of the procurement records.

3.7 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant deduct warranty security in an amount equivalent to at least one percent (1%) but not to exceed five percent (5%) of the purchase price or the supplier to pose a special bank guarantee equivalent to at least one percent (1%) but not to exceed five percent (5%) of the total contract price to cover the warranty security.

4.8 We recommended that management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Engineer to ensure that all programs/projects/activities funded under the 20% Development Fund are implemented in the year they were appropriated to ensure the fund’s optimum utilization; and
b. Address location issues of development projects before their inclusion in the annual development fund budget to facilitate timely implementation.

5.6 We recommended and management agreed to require the winning bidders to extend the Performance Bonds of the infrastructure projects awarded to them to cover any additional contract time by securing a written consent from the insurance company and attach the same to the approved extension period and/or variation orders submitted to the Audit Team.

6.5 We recommended that the Accounting and Budget Office promptly establish a Responsibility Center then assign RC codes or if impractical to establish a separate RC, shall maintain subsidiary ledgers/registries to record all GAD-related expenses and financial transactions, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2021-008, in order to facilitate the monitoring, accounting and preparation of the GAD-related reports.

7.6 We recommended that Management ensure that suspensions are settled within the reglementary period pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

7.7 We further recommended the immediate submission of the unsubmitted documents required in the above Notices of Suspension. Failure to comply may compel the audit team to issue Notices of Disallowance for the related transactions, given the prolonged period of non-compliance.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements information pertaining to DRRM not evident on the face of the Financial Statements such as those enumerated in Item VII.2 of COA – Circular 2014-002.

observations.recommendation:
1.4 We recommended that Management instruct the:
Municipal Treasurer to:
a. submit to the accounting the unsubmitted official receipts identified in
paragraph 1.3.2 of this report for appropriate recording;
Municipal Accountant to:
b. effect the proposed adjusting journal entries in Annex E of this report to
establish the correct balances of affected accounts;
c. provide the appropriate depreciation expense on all depreciable Property
Plant and Equipment;
d. properly monitor the general ledger of the Accounts Payable account for
easier detection of errors and ensure that obligations presented in the
financial statement are supported by a valid claim.;
e. ensure that the recording of financial transactions is made in accordance
with the prescribed entries provided under relevant accounting policies so
that the account balances in the financial statements are accurately
presented; and
f. prepare and maintain the subsidiary ledgers of each account for easier
monitoring and detection of errors and furnish copies to the Audit Team.

1.5 We further recommended that the General Services Officer/Supply Officer in
coordination with the other concerned offices conduct an inventory of all the
infrastructure assets of the municipality, maintain the required property cards,
prepare the inventory report and furnish a copy to the audit team.

2.19 We recommended that management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:
a. require the revenue collectors to remit their collections daily or on a weekly
basis or upon reaching the amount of ₱10,000.00 to avoid misuse or loss of
government funds;
b. ensure that accountable officers/collectors in the Municipal Treasury Office
report all collections for recording and that remittances are deposited intact; and,
c. issue only additional booklet of official receipts to collectors once the
collection from previously issued booklets are remitted in full.

2.20 We likewise recommended that management demand from the concerned
collectors the remittance of collections from the previously issued booklets
not yet remitted as of December 31, 2023 and demand the presentation and
remittance of collections, if any, of the unpresented official receipts.

3.8 We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer constantly monitor the
management of collections by directing:
a. the accountable officers to issue and record official receipts in strict
numerical sequence pursuant to Section 81.E of the Local Treasury
Operations Manual, Book 2; and
b. both the collector passing the unused official receipts and the receiving
collector, if warranted, to record in their respective cashbook the transfer of
the unused official receipts in order to establish proper accountability of
collections.

4.18 We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. issue demand letters to those employees with unliquidated and due cash
advances, otherwise, cause the withholding of the Accountable Officer’s
salary until full settlement of cash advance is made;
b. strictly enforce the liquidation of cash advances within the reglementary
period and require the accountable officer to refund the unspent amount of
their cash advances upon submission of liquidation documents; and
c. conduct regular and periodic verification of loan receivable accounts to
determine its conditions that would affect its collectability.

4.19 We also recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer and
concerned offices to institute appropriate collection procedures such as the
issuance of demand letters to recover the non-moving dormant accounts.

4.20 We further recommended that Management requests for write-off the account
balances that are considered dormant or determined to be non-collectable
pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.

5.13 We recommended that management instruct the concerned office/personnel to
properly monitor and expedite the utilization of funds received from other
agencies.

6.12 We recommended that Management:
a. Instruct the Municipal Engineer to facilitate the completion of the
programs/projects/activities under the 20% Development Fund in the year
they were appropriated to ensure the fund’s optimum utilization; and
a. Instruct the project engineers/inspectors concerned to regularly and
thoroughly monitor and supervise all projects to ensure that construction
works are accomplished in accordance with the terms of the contract.

7.6 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to record and transfer
immediately at year-end the cash corresponding to the unexpended balance of
the LDRRMF for QRF and MOOE from the General Fund to the Trust Fund
account.

8.12 We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. implement all programs and projects under the LDRRM fund whether from
current, from prior years or continuing appropriation, otherwise, if not
needed revert to the unexpended portion for re-programming to other
activities or projects;
b. instruct the concerned personnel to perform regular monitoring and
evaluation of the programs, projects and activities (PPAs) vis-à-vis targets to
ensure the attainment of the municipality’s goals and objectives on disaster
response and mitigation; and
c. instruct the concerned municipal officials to facilitate the completion of the
infrastructure projects under the LDRRM fund and to regularly and
thoroughly monitor and supervise all projects to ensure that construction
works are accomplished in accordance with the terms of the contract.

9.14 We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to coordinate with the
Municipal Engineering Office to strictly observe proper procedures required
under Annex E and Annex I of the 2016 Implementing Rules and Regulations of
RA No. 9184 and DPWH Department Order No. 193 Series 2016 specifically on
the issuance of warning and termination of contracts for projects incurring
negative slippages to avoid too much delay in the implementation of projects.

10.10 In view of the foregoing, we recommended that management instruct the Bids and
Awards Committee (BAC)-Technical Working Group to thoroughly verify, validate,
and ascertain the performance of the winning bidder in its on-going government and
private contract and if due to said bidder’s fault or negligence, it has incurred a
negative slippage of at least fifteen percent (15%) in any one project or a negative
slippage of at least ten percent (10%) in each of two (2) or more contracts, the BAC
shall recommend the disqualification of the winning bidder from the award, and
undertake the procedures provided in Sections 34 and 35 of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
a. effect the proposed adjusting journal entries (Annex B, PAJE Nos.1-12) to establish the correct balances of affected accounts;
b. ensure that the recording of financial transactions is made in accordance with the prescribed entries provided under relevant accounting policies so that the account balances in the financial statements are accurately presented; and
c. prepare and maintain the subsidiary ledgers of each account for easier monitoring and detection of errors and furnish copies to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management should:

Instruct all accountable officers, officials and employees with cash advances that are due and demandable to submit their liquidation reports for proper accounting;

Require all officers and employees with unliquidated cash advances in excess of the actual amount spent to immediately refund the excess to the Revenue Collecting Officer of the LGU, otherwise, deduct the amount from salaries.

Exert all possible efforts to require the settlement of the outstanding cash advance from those resigned/separated officials and employees. If liquidation is not possible, request for write off of accounts receivable from the Commission Proper of the Commission on Audit.

We recommended that Management should stop the practice of granting additional cash advance to officers and employees with existing cash advance.

Further, we recommended that appropriate sanction like deduction of long outstanding cash advances from salaries should be imposed to officers and employees who continuously violate Section 89 of PD 1445.
We have recommended that Management  submit justification for granting a Cash advance which is covered by an insufficient fidelity bond.
We recommended that Management submit the lacking requirements enumerated above  to prove the regularity of disbursements.
We have recommended that Management  submit complete documentation for all cash advances granted and liquidations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit all the supporting documents required in the various government transactions in compliance to Section 4.6 of PD 1445, COA Circulars 2012-001 and 2004-006 to avoid possible suspensions and disallowances.

We recommended that Management should require the responsible officials to immediately submit all the vouchers for immediate review and verification and to enable the Audit Team to inform deficiencies noted for appropriate action.

We have recommended that Management: 

immediately remit the amount of ₱2,054,978.60 to the BIR to avoid possible penalty that may be imposed.



 regularly update its subsidiary ledgers in prior years to determine  unremitted taxes, and henceforth, remit taxes withheld within ten (10) days after the end of each month as required under BIR Regulation No. 2-98.

We recommended that Management require the Accounting Office to reconcile their records with the GSIS, PAG-IBIG and PHILHEALTH and remit immediately all outstanding accounts with them.

 

We further recommended that Management should remit in full the monthly contributions and loan amortizations withheld and, henceforth, strictly comply with the aforementioned laws and regulations on the remittances of mandatory obligations to ensure that employees will continue to enjoy the benefits from these agencies.

We recommended that Management:

submit the following documents/reports for evaluation/verification:
2014 LDRRMFIP 
Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF; and
Notes to the Financial Statement for the details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF. 

require the LDRRMO to strictly comply with the provision of COA Circular No. 2012-002 particularly on the timely preparation and submission of reports.

require the Municipal Accountant to properly disclose in  the LGU’s notes to the financial statements, the amount and details of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF,  for the succeeding reports.

Considering the control weaknesses noted, we recommended:

The accountable officers and the Municipal Accountant conduct reconciliation of records from time to time to prevent and detect any errors that might occur and correction shall be made immediately. Also, references shall be indicated on all records in both the cash book and accounting records including liquidation reports to properly match cash advances and liquidations and prevent any occurrence of errors.

The Municipal Accountant  establish the subsidiary ledgers for the special cash advances accounts needed in the conduct of reconciliation and verification of existing cash advances due for liquidation. Any unliquidated cash advances due for liquidation shall be demanded immediately and further cash advances shall not be allowed unless full liquidation has been made.

The accountant book up the proposed adjusting entries to correct the misstatements of accounts.

We recommended that:

Management adhere strictly with the provisions of RA 9184 in the conduct of their procurement activities for succeeding transactions.





Management should enter into loan agreement with KAELCO setting forth the principal amount of loan, the terms and period of payment, interest, including terms and conditions for the maintenance and repair of the project in cases of breakdown.


The BAC and management should justify the procurement of materials from a business whose line of business is electricity distribution and not supply and delivery of electrical materials. The BAC should also justify the financial capability of KAELCO to participate in the bidding to supply such materials as it contradicts the MOA signed by Balbalan & KAELCO.





The BAC and management should justify the awarding of contract for the installation of electrical posts and lines instead of tapping the services of KAELCO considering that such is within the capability of the cooperative and being the one who would be benefitted from the income derived from the project.  


Management should submit all justifications and documentary deficiencies noted and such other documents which would enlighten us as to the regularity and lawfulness of said disbursements and to enable us to make a decision in audit.
observations.managementAction: The BAC Chairman and its members submitted their justification on the procurement of materials and awarding of contract dated March 27, 2013.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that responsible officials should immediately stop the granting of salary loans to LGU employees; and the Treasurer or Collecting Officer should collect all unpaid balances, interests and surcharges, if any, to be reverted back to the general fund.
